glossary.GCID: Group Customer ID — cross-product identity key linking the same person across eToro products/entities. Introduced after launch; older accounts may have NULL GCID. gcid=0 indicates omnibus/system wallets (not real customers).
glossary.ActionTypeId: Transaction direction in WalletDB: 1=Sent (outgoing), 2=Recive (incoming). Note: 'Recive' is a legacy misspelling in WalletDB source, preserved across EXW_dbo for backward compatibility.
glossary.TransactionTypeId_sent: Sent transaction type in WalletDB: 0=Redeem, 1=CustomerMoneyOut, 2=AmlMoneyBack, 4=Funding, 5=ConversionMoneyIn, 6=ConversionMoneyOut, 7=Payment, 8=RedeemAsic, 9=Staking, 10=BlockChainActivation, 11=OmnibusMoneyOut, 12=ConversionToFiat, 13=ManualUserMoneyOut, 14=StakeAndRewardsRefund, 15=CustomerMoneyBack. FK to WalletDB.Dictionary.TransactionTypes.
glossary.EtoroFees_pre_multiplied: EtoroFees in EXW_FactTransactions = source.EtoroFees × FeeExchangeRate. Do NOT re-multiply — the rate has already been applied. For most types FeeExchangeRate=1; ConversionOut and Payment differ.
glossary.Generic_Pipeline_Bronze: WalletDB data exported to Bronze ADLS Parquet via Generic Pipeline (ADF/CopyFromLake). Override strategy, every 60 minutes. Synapse External Tables read these Parquet files directly via internal-sources data source.
glossary.RealCID: Customer ID — platform-internal primary key assigned at registration. Universal customer identifier across all eToro DWH tables.
glossary.EXW_DimUser: Primary Wallet user dimension. Scoped to EXW_Wallet.CustomerWalletsView (Wallet users only). 699,692 rows. HASH(GCID), CCI.
glossary.CustomerWalletsView: EXW_Wallet view that defines the scope of Wallet users — the canonical source of which GCIDs are active Wallet users.
glossary.CopyFromLake: Synapse schema housing external tables sourced from the data lake (Bronze layer). Used for WalletDB data: WalletDB_Wallet_V_BI_WalletBalances, WalletDB_Wallet_TermsAndConditions, WalletDB_Wallet_CustomerTermsAndConditions.
glossary.TypeID_WalletRegulation: Regulatory T&C type: 1=eToroX, 2=US, 3=Germany, 4=eToro DA, 5=eToro SEY. Hardcoded in SP_EXW_WalletRegulation.
glossary.ProviderUserID: Base64-encoded GCID string used as external identifier for AML compliance providers. Padding ('=') stripped in ProviderUserIDNormalized.
glossary.IsCurrent_WalletRegulation: 1 = user's active regulation (ToDate='2999-01-01'); 0 = immediately prior regulation period.
glossary.SP_EXW_UserSettingsWalletAllowance_dual_: SP_EXW_UserSettingsWalletAllowance writes BOTH EXW_UserSettingsWalletAllowance (lines 380–406) AND EXW_AML_Users_Report (lines 780–837) in a single run. A failure affects both tables.
glossary.IsAMLProblematic: Composite AML flag on EXW_AML_Users_Report. 1 if any of 7 conditions: PlayerStatusID NOT IN (5,1,12); CountryRankID≠0; ScreeningStatusID NOT IN (0,1); RiskScoreName NOT IN (Low,Medium); RiskClassificationID NOT IN (1,2); UserWalletAllowance≠Allowed; Age≤25 or Age≥65.
glossary.EXW_Settings_ResourceId_5903: EXW_Settings.SystemRestrictions ResourceId=5903 = Wallet allowance settings. ResourceId=35467 = customer-level individual overrides.
glossary.CountryRankID: Dim_Country.RiskGroupID for country risk ranking: 0=Open, 1=No Business Allowed, 2=Open For Existing Customers, 3=High Risk EDD. NULL=NA.
glossary.WalletPool: EXW_Wallet.WalletPool — pool of pre-funded wallets assigned to customers. WalletId (GUID) is the business key. Pool wallets are pre-funded before customer assignment. IsFunded/IsAssigned track lifecycle state.
glossary.EXW_Price: EXW_Wallet.EXW_Price — daily crypto-to-USD price table. Join: CryptoId + (TransDate > DateFrom AND TransDate <= DateTo). Used by SP_EXW_Fact_Transactions to compute AmountUSD, EtoroFeesUSD, BlockchainFeesUSD.
glossary.AmlValidations: EXW_Wallet.AmlValidations — AML provider decisions per transaction. Values: Green (clear), Amber (review), Red (flagged), NA (not applicable), Error. Joined by CorrelationId (sent, TypeId=1 only) or BlockchainTransactionId+WalletId (received). NULL = no AML check performed (~69% of EXW_FactTransactions rows).
glossary.EXW_Aml_Limited_Accounts_frozen: EXW_dbo.EXW_Aml_Limited_Accounts is frozen as of 2023-11-13 (3,650 rows). The live AML-limited accounts feed is BI_DB_dbo.External_Fivetran_google_sheets_exw_aml_limited_accounts. Both are unioned by SP_EXW_CompensationClosingCountries to build the complete AML-limited user population (#Aml_Limited).
glossary.EXW_ReimbursementSumTable_7rows: EXW_dbo.EXW_ReimbursementSumTable always contains exactly 7 rows — one per population segment in the AML compensation universe. Written by the final section of SP_EXW_CompensationClosingCountries (TRUNCATE + INSERT). [Compensated By Current USD Price] is the current market value of compensation crypto (FinalBalance × current price), NOT the original payment amount.
glossary.EXW_Coin_Transfer_Allowed_Country_matrix: EXW_dbo.EXW_Coin_Transfer_Allowed_Country is the redemption eligibility matrix (1.74M rows). [Coin Transfer Allowed]=1 if LOWER(SelectedValue)='true'. Written by SP_EXW_WalletElligibleCountries (ResourceId=5903, 'redeem/allow' family). Priority hierarchy: CountryRegionAndRegulation > CountryAndRegion > PlayerLevelAndCountry > Country > RegulationGroup > GeoRegistrationDate > Default.
glossary.AML_Projects_current_vs_legacy: SP_EXW_CompensationClosingCountries manages only AML, AML_US, AML_EEA projects (current). 15 additional legacy project values (FrenchTerr=51K rows, Germany_Tangany_AirDrop=47K, Russia=17K, Netherlands=8K, etc.) represent 98% of EXW_CompensationClosingCountries rows and were loaded by prior ETL (mechanism unknown from current SSDT code).
glossary.SP_EXW_CompensationClosingCountries_trip: SP_EXW_CompensationClosingCountries writes THREE tables in a single run: EXW_CompensationClosingCountries (UPSERT), EXW_ReimbursementFollowUp (TRUNCATE+INSERT), and EXW_ReimbursementSumTable (TRUNCATE+INSERT). A failure mid-run can leave the tables in inconsistent states.
glossary.EXW_ReimbursementFollowUp_space_columns: EXW_dbo.EXW_ReimbursementFollowUp has 13 of 56 columns with spaces in their names — all must use bracket-quoting. Critical: `[Date Rate For  Reimbursement]` has a DOUBLE SPACE between 'For' and 'Reimbursement'. WalletVsPlatform classifies reconciliation gaps (6 values). GCID-level aggregates (PlatformUSDCompensationPerGCID, WalletDataUSDReimbursementPerGCID) are repeated across all CryptoId rows for the same GCID.
glossary.SelectedValue_wallet_allowance: SelectedValue in EXW_UserSettingsWalletAllowance: 0=Closed, 1=ReadOnly, 2=Allowed, 3=AllowedForExistingOnly. In EXW_Coin_Transfer_Allowed_Country: SelectedValue is 'true'/'false' string (different encoding). Do not confuse the two.
glossary.RestrictionWeight_priority: EXW_Settings.SystemRestrictions.RestrictionWeight: higher value = higher priority. When multiple tags match the same (country, playerLevel, regulation, crypto) combination, the tag with the max RestrictionWeight wins and determines SelectedValue.
glossary.EXW_FinanceReportsBalancesNew_reference_: EXW_dbo.EXW_FinanceReportsBalancesNew serves as the balance reference for multiple downstream consumers. SP_EXW_CompensationClosingCountries uses MAX(BalanceDateID) from this table as @d for both balance and price lookups.
glossary.GCIDUnion_omnibus_resolution: GCIDUnion in EXW_EthFeeSent_Blockchain = CASE WHEN GCID>0 THEN GCID ELSE CustomerWalletsView.Gcid (receiver via ReciverAddress=Address). Resolves omnibus-sender transactions (GCID=0 in EXW_FactTransactions) to the actual user GCID for country/regulation attribution.
glossary.EXW_EthFeeSent_Blockchain_activity: Activity column in EXW_EthFeeSent_Blockchain: 'User Send Out'=134,697 (39.8%), 'Coin Transfer'=131,642 (38.9%), 'Wallet Creation'=25,713 (7.6%), 'Not Exist on Wallet'=8,978 (2.7%). Wallet Creation detected via contract_address IS NOT NULL OR method='Create Wallet'.
glossary.EXW_Transactions_Monthly_deprecated: EXW_dbo.EXW_Transactions_Monthly is DEPRECATED — SP body is entirely commented out (NO-OP). Data frozen at 2023-12-31. 50.1M rows, 489K GCIDs, 69 months. Use EXW_FactTransactions for post-2023 monthly analysis.
glossary.EXW_UserCalculatedBalance_deprecated: EXW_dbo.EXW_UserCalculatedBalance is DEPRECATED — SP body is entirely commented out (NO-OP). Data frozen at 2023-12-31. 1.27 BILLION rows. Balance = cumulative ReceivedAmount-SentAmount-(0.0225 XRP reserve). Superseded by EXW_FinanceReportsBalancesNew (direct snapshot).
glossary.EXW_30DayBalanceExtract_enrichments: EXW_dbo.EXW_30DayBalanceExtract enriches EXW_FinanceReportsBalancesNew with 4 additional columns from EXW_DimUser: Region, StateCode (UserRegionID), State (UserRegion_State), ComplianceClosureEvent. CryptoId/CryptoName = blockchain-level (vs ERC in source table). TRUNCATE+INSERT, rolling 31-day window.
glossary.New_UsersAndWallets_Inventory_join_dates: EXW_dbo.New_UsersAndWallets_Inventory: WalletJoinDate = MIN(EXW_WalletInventory.Allocated) per GCID×CryptoId — earliest wallet allocation date for that crypto. UserJoinDate = MIN(EXW_WalletInventory.Allocated) per GCID — user's first-ever wallet date across all cryptos. WHERE GCID>0 filters out unallocated pool wallets. Used by SP_EXW_FirstTimeWalletsAndUsers as the source of first-time wallet/user dates.
glossary.SP_EXW_Hourly_multi_writer: SP_EXW_Hourly writes 6 tables in a single run (all TRUNCATE+INSERT): Hourly_CustomerBalances (4-day rolling), Hourly_OmnibusBalances (4-day rolling), Hourly_RedeemActivity (7-day rolling), Hourly_WalletInventory, Hourly_WalletAllocations (7-day rolling), Hourly_Transactions (5-day rolling). Designed to run hourly. @d DATE param is accepted but not used — all dates computed from GETDATE().
glossary.Hourly_CustomerBalances_anomaly: Hourly_CustomerBalances today-2 snapshot is missing the Balance <> 0 filter present in today, today-1, and today-3 snapshots. Zero-balance wallets are included in today-2 records only.
glossary.Hourly_Transactions_ReciverAddress_typo: Hourly_Transactions.ReciverAddress is a persistent typo ('Reciver', missing one 'e') inherited from External_WalletDB_Wallet_TransactionsView. Present in DDL and SP — bracket-quoting with the misspelled form is mandatory.
glossary.EXW_ETH_FeeData_Blockchain_varchar_casts: EXW_dbo.EXW_ETH_FeeData_Blockchain stores all 18 columns as nvarchar/varchar. Numeric columns (txn_fee_eth, txn_fee_usd, etc.) require CAST(CAST(col AS FLOAT) AS MONEY) due to scientific notation in source. Columns `from` and `to` are reserved SQL keywords — bracket-quoting mandatory.
glossary.EXW_WalletElligibleCountries_TagType_202: EXW_dbo.EXW_WalletElligibleCountries: new TagType='CountryRegionAndRegulation' added 2026-04-14 to SP_EXW_WalletElligibleCountries. Priority resolution: Max(RestrictionWeight) per CountryID×RegulationID×RegionByIP_ID. TagType='Default' means no specific rule configured (fallback SelectedValue applies).
glossary.StateCode_inconsistency: StateCode encoding differs between EXW tables: EXW_FirstTimeWalletsAndUsers.StateCode = DWH_dbo.Dim_State_and_Province.ShortName (e.g., 'NY'); EXW_30DayBalanceExtract.StateCode = EXW_DimUser.UserRegionID (integer region code as varchar). Do not cross-join these StateCode columns directly.
glossary.Hourly_WalletAllocations_AllocationDate_: Hourly_WalletAllocations.AllocationDate is hardcoded to CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE) in SP_EXW_Hourly — it always equals ReportDate and does NOT contain the wallet allocation date. Use CAST(Occurred AS DATE) to get the actual allocation date.
glossary.WalletPool_lifecycle_statuses: WalletPool lifecycle statuses (from WalletDB_Dictionary_WalletPoolStatuses): Verified=active operational state (dominant, ~95% of wallets), FundingVerified=pre-funded with gas ETH (ETH wallets only — required for ERC-20 operations), Pending=creation in progress, Failed=creation failed, VerifiedForAssign=ready for special assignment. Only ETH has FundingVerified rows.
glossary.CrytpoType_typo_WalletAllocations: Hourly_WalletAllocations.CrytpoType column name has a typo ('CrytpoType' — letters transposed) baked into DDL and SP. Always use the misspelled form in queries. Values: 'MainCryptos' (native coins: CryptoID=BlockchainCryptoId) and 'ERCCryptos' (ERC-20 tokens: CryptoID≠BlockchainCryptoId).
glossary.Hourly_7day_window_formula: SP_EXW_Hourly uses Convert(DateTime, DATEDIFF(DAY, 7, GETDATE())) for the 7-day lookback in Hourly_RedeemActivity and Hourly_WalletAllocations. This unusual expression (originally GETDATE()-7) has confirmed 7-day behavior based on live data. The formula works by: DATEDIFF(DAY, 7, GETDATE()) converts '7' to datetime (1900-01-08) then counts days to today → a large integer → Convert(DateTime, large_int) maps back to approximately today-7.
glossary.Hourly_WalletInventory_native_only: Hourly_WalletInventory covers ONLY native coin wallets. ERC-20 tokens are excluded by WHERE CryptoID = BlockchainCryptoId in the final INSERT. Contrast: Hourly_WalletAllocations includes both native and ERC-20 wallets (no such filter).
glossary.CustomerWalletsView_T1_for_WalletAllocat: 12 of 16 Hourly_WalletAllocations columns are Tier 1 from WalletDB.Wallet.CustomerWalletsView upstream wiki: ID, GCID, CryptoID, Address, BlockchainProviderWalletId, Occurred, WalletTypeId, IsActive, Status, WalletRecordId, BlockchainCryptoId, WalletProviderId. This is the highest T1 coverage of any EXW_dbo Hourly table.
glossary.ETH_staking_program_frozen: All four EXW_dbo Staking_* tables (Staking_ETH_Rewards_Parameters, Staking_WalletUserRewards, Staking_BI_Version_WalletUserRewards, Staking_BI_Version_ETH_Transactions) are frozen historical archives — the ETH staking program ended May 2023. No new rows have been added since then. No SP writer exists in SSDT for any of these tables; all are populated by an unknown external ETL (Databricks/ADF) via EXW_Staking External Tables (Bronze layer).
glossary.Staking_BI_vs_base: Staking_BI_Version_WalletUserRewards (23,659 rows) differs from Staking_WalletUserRewards (23,617 rows): the BI version has 42 extra rows (IsTestUser=1 test accounts), covers fewer months (Jul 2021–May 2023 vs Jun 2021–Jun 2023), uses uniqueidentifier for WalletID (vs varchar), numeric(2,2) for ClubRevShare (vs varchar), and float for MonthlyRewards/EligibleRewards (vs decimal).
glossary.Staking_transaction_vs_summary_grain: Staking_BI_Version_ETH_Transactions (transaction grain: one row per staking operation) is the position-level detail that underpins Staking_BI_Version_WalletUserRewards (user-month summary grain). SUM(ClientMonthlyStakingReward) in ETH_Transactions per GCID+StakingMonthID should equal MonthlyRewards in WalletUserRewards. COUNT(*) per GCID+StakingMonthID equals EligibleTransactions in WalletUserRewards.
glossary.EXW_InternalWallet_gcid_filter: EXW_dbo.EXW_InternalWallet contains only internal/system wallets (Gcid<=0). Gcid=0 = omnibus/system wallets (eToro's pooled holdings); Gcid<0 = internal service account wallets. Customer wallets (Gcid>0, WalletTypeId=5) are excluded by SP filter. WalletTypes present: 1=Redeem, 2=Conversion, 3=Funding, 4=Payment, 6=C2F, 7=StakingRefund.
glossary.EXW_InternalWallet_UpdateDate_gotcha: EXW_InternalWallet.UpdateDate maps to CustomerWalletsView.Occurred (= WalletAssets.Occurred = when the crypto asset was first added to the wallet). Despite the name 'UpdateDate', it is a wallet creation/association timestamp — NOT the ETL run time. This is a misleading column name inherited from the SP.
glossary.EXW_WalletLogins_scope: EXW_dbo.EXW_WalletLogins contains only login events for wallet users (HasWallet=1 in Dim_Customer), sourced from Fact_CustomerAction (ActionTypeID=14, LoggedIn). ApplicationIdentifier is always 'retoro' (hardcoded in SP). EnvironmentDetails is always NULL. Delete-insert by @date (idempotent). SP_WalletLogins parameterized by @date.
glossary.Staking_CorrelationID_idempotency: In EXW_dbo.Staking_BI_Version_ETH_Transactions, CorrelationID maps to WalletDB.Staking.Staking.CorrelationId — the idempotency key used by Staking.InsertStaking to prevent duplicate staking operations (NOT EXISTS check before insert). Preserved in DWH for cross-system reconciliation.
glossary.Simplex_UTI_chain: UTI (Unique Transaction Identifier) is the cross-reference key linking the Simplex payment ecosystem: EXW_SimplexMapping.long_id = EXW_ECPBank.uti = EXW_PaymentReconciliation.UTI. EXW_ECPBank.merch_tran_ref_ = first 15 chars of UTI. EXW_SimplexChargebacks.ARN cross-references via EXW_ECPBank.acquirer_ref_ (23-digit ARN). Use UTI as the join key across all three tables.
glossary.Simplex_ecosystem_frozen: All 5 Simplex-era EXW_dbo tables are frozen: EXW_SimplexMapping (frozen 2022-09-19), EXW_SimplexChargebacks (5 rows, one-time load 2020-03-15), EXW_ECPBank (frozen 2022-09-20), EXW_FactPayments (frozen 2022-09-20), EXW_PaymentReconciliation (frozen 2022-09-20). Simplex decommissioned as eToro's crypto buy provider ~September 2022. No new rows will ever be added.
glossary.EXW_FactPayments_accumulating_snapshot: EXW_FactPayments stores one row per (PaymentID × status event) = 553,884 rows for 99,410 distinct PaymentIDs (~5.57 rows per payment). The 11-stage status lifecycle: InitiateStarted → PendingProvider → InitiateCompleted → ProviderSubmitted → PendingTransaction → TransferCompleted → Completed (plus failures: Failed, InternalError, InitiateFailed, DocumentCompleted). Filter to PaymentStatus='Completed' for successful payments. Use MAX(ModificationDate) per PaymentID for latest status.
glossary.EXW_PaymentReconciliation_final_state: EXW_PaymentReconciliation is the final-state complement to EXW_FactPayments: one row per PaymentID at its last known status (99,243 rows = 99,243 distinct PaymentIDs). Joins WalletDB payment data (LEFT JOIN EXW_SimplexMapping via UTI — 38,044 matched) + EXW_ECPBank (LEFT JOIN via UTI — 20,944 matched). ECPAmout column name has DDL typo (missing 'n') — use exactly 'ECPAmout' in all queries.
glossary.EXW_ECPBank_fivetran: EXW_ECPBank was loaded by Fivetran from ECP Bank (card acquirer, Gibraltar merchant). _row, _fivetran_deleted, _fivetran_synced are Fivetran-synthetic. transaction_date (authorization) is NULL for post-2020 records; posting_date (settlement) always present. merchant_no_ has 3 format variants (all same Gibraltar merchant: 172000000006524). acquirer_bin_ica: 453760=Visa, 14206=Mastercard.
glossary.EXW_WalletEntity_legal_entity: EXW_dbo.EXW_WalletEntity assigns each wallet customer to one of 8 eToro legal entities (EtoroX, eToro SEY, eToro EU, eToro UK, etc.) via a CASE branch in SP_EXW_WalletEntity. Entity assignment sourced from BI_DB_dbo.External_WalletDB_Dictionary_EtoroLegalEntities. CountryID=169 excluded from entity assignment (hardcoded). JoinDate = MIN(CustomerWalletsView.Occurred), NOT the eToro platform registration date. TermsAndConditionTime is a full datetime despite the name.
glossary.EXW_FCA_UserLogin_name_misnomer: EXW_dbo.EXW_FCA_UserLogin is a misnomer — it contains login events for ALL wallet users (HasWallet=1), not only FCA-regulated users. Sourced from Fact_CustomerAction (ActionTypeID=14, LoggedIn). SP_EXW_FCA_UserLogin runs daily by @date parameter (DELETE+INSERT pattern).

cross_references.EXW_DimUser_is_hub: EXW_DimUser is the central dimension — EXW_AMLProviderID, EXW_FactBalance, EXW_WalletRegulation, EXW_DimUser_Enriched, EXW_UserSettingsWalletAllowance, and EXW_AML_Users_Report all JOIN to it for GCID scope.
cross_references.EXW_DimUser_Enriched_is_secondary_hub: EXW_DimUser_Enriched aggregates 12 sources (incl. Dim_Customer, V_Liabilities, KYC) and feeds EXW_AML_Users_Report as its primary enrichment source.
cross_references.CustomerWalletsView_scope: EXW_Wallet.CustomerWalletsView defines the Wallet user population across EXW_DimUser, EXW_FactBalance, EXW_WalletRegulation, and EXW_WalletInventory.
cross_references.RealCID_T1_chain: RealCID is T1 (Customer.CustomerStatic) in EXW_DimUser, EXW_AMLProviderID, EXW_FactBalance, EXW_WalletRegulation, EXW_DimUser_Enriched, EXW_UserSettingsWalletAllowance, and EXW_AML_Users_Report.
cross_references.EXW_WalletClosedCountryProjects_feeds_Di: EXW_WalletClosedCountryProjects is the source for EXW_DimUser.ComplianceClosureEvent and EXW_UserSettingsWalletAllowance.ComplianceClosureEvent.
cross_references.EXW_TestUsers_feeds_DimUser: EXW_TestUsers is the source for EXW_DimUser.IsTestAccount via LEFT JOIN in SP_DimUser.
cross_references.SP_dual_writer: SP_EXW_UserSettingsWalletAllowance writes BOTH EXW_UserSettingsWalletAllowance and EXW_AML_Users_Report. Both share the same refresh dependency.
cross_references.EXW_AML_Users_Report_not_1to1: EXW_AML_Users_Report has 699,852 rows but 699,692 distinct GCIDs — 160 GCIDs appear twice due to EXW_AMLProviderID fan-out. Always use COUNT(DISTINCT GCID) on this table.
cross_references.Bronze_External_pipeline: External_WalletDB_Wallet_TransactionsView reads Bronze Parquet via Generic Pipeline (Override, 60-min cadence). UC Target = wallet.bronze_walletdb_wallet_transactionsview (already in Unity Catalog as Bronze Delta). The same data is accessible in both Synapse (External Table) and UC (Delta).
cross_references.External_feeds_FactTransactions: External_WalletDB_Wallet_TransactionsView is the sole Bronze staging source for EXW_FactTransactions. SP_EXW_Fact_Transactions DELETEs and re-INSERTs rows for a target date, reading all 22 source columns and enriching with 23 DWH-computed columns.
cross_references.EXW_FactConversions_deprecated: EXW_FactConversions covers crypto-to-crypto conversions — a feature deprecated post-June 2023. It is a historical one-time load (50K rows); no ongoing ETL. SP_US_Daily_Crypto in BI_DB reads it via ToEtoroSentTXID JOIN to EXW_FactTransactions.
cross_references.WalletInventory_PublicAddress_link: EXW_WalletInventory.PublicAddress matches SenderAddress in EXW_FactTransactions for sent transactions — the wallet's on-chain address is the physical link between pool state and transaction history.
cross_references.SP_CompensationClosingCountries_triple_o: SP_EXW_CompensationClosingCountries produces three tables in one run: EXW_CompensationClosingCountries (UPSERT), EXW_ReimbursementFollowUp (TRUNCATE+INSERT), EXW_ReimbursementSumTable (TRUNCATE+INSERT). A single SP failure can leave all three in inconsistent states.
cross_references.AML_limited_dual_source: EXW_Aml_Limited_Accounts (frozen 2023-11) + BI_DB_dbo.External_Fivetran_google_sheets_exw_aml_limited_accounts (live) are UNION-ed in SP_EXW_CompensationClosingCountries to build #Aml_Limited for EXW_ReimbursementSumTable segment classification.
cross_references.EXW_FinanceReportsBalancesNew_balance_re: EXW_FinanceReportsBalancesNew is the balance reference for multiple downstream consumers: EXW_DimUser_Enriched (TotalBalanceUSD), EXW_ReimbursementSumTable (BalanceUSD via @d=MAX(BalanceDateID)), EXW_ReimbursementFollowUp ('Date of Current User Balance').
cross_references.EXW_CompensationClosingCountries_feeds_F: EXW_CompensationClosingCountries is read by SP_EXW_FinanceReportsBalancesNew for AMLClosureEvent condition 4 (WHERE GCID IN SELECT GCID FROM EXW_CompensationClosingCountries AND SelectedValue=0).
cross_references.EXW_Coin_Transfer_reads_CryptoTypes: EXW_Coin_Transfer_Allowed_Country sources InstrumentID, CryptoID, and Crypto directly from EXW_Wallet.CryptoTypes — the only EXW_dbo table in Batch 4 with T1 columns. EXW_Wallet.CryptoTypes mirrors Wallet.CryptoTypes (WalletDB wiki available).
cross_references.WalletElligibleCountries_vs_CoinTransfer: SP_EXW_WalletElligibleCountries populates both EXW_WalletElligibleCountries (wallet open/close eligibility, one row per country+regulation) and EXW_Coin_Transfer_Allowed_Country (redemption eligibility matrix, one row per country+playerLevel+regulation+crypto combination). Same SP, different resource sections.
cross_references.SP_EXW_Hourly_6table_writer: SP_EXW_Hourly writes 6 tables in a single run: Hourly_CustomerBalances, Hourly_OmnibusBalances, Hourly_RedeemActivity, Hourly_WalletInventory, Hourly_WalletAllocations, Hourly_Transactions. All 6 are TRUNCATE+INSERT. A SP failure mid-run leaves some tables current and others stale.
cross_references.New_UsersAndWallets_Inventory_feeds_Firs: New_UsersAndWallets_Inventory.WalletJoinDate and UserJoinDate are the direct input to SP_EXW_FirstTimeWalletsAndUsers — the source of first-time wallet registration dates in EXW_FirstTimeWalletsAndUsers.
cross_references.EXW_ETH_FeeData_Blockchain_feeds_EthFeeS: EXW_ETH_FeeData_Blockchain feeds SP_EXW_EthFeeSent_Blockchain (documented Batch 5). EXW_ETH_FeeData_Blockchain stores raw Etherscan fee data; EXW_EthFeeSent_Blockchain enriches with GCID and country/regulation attribution.
cross_references.EXW_ReimbursementFollowUp_extraction_cut: EXW_ReimbursementFollowUp TotalExtracted* columns reflect EXW_FactTransactions TransactionTypeID=13 extractions since 2024-01-18 only. This same cutoff does NOT appear in EXW_EthFeeSent_Blockchain or EXW_FactTransactions — it is specific to the reimbursement extraction tracking logic.
cross_references.Hourly_OmnibusBalances_CustomerBalances_: Hourly_OmnibusBalances (Gcid<=0, per-wallet) and Hourly_CustomerBalances (Gcid>0, per-crypto aggregate) are complementary counterparts written in the same SP_EXW_Hourly run. Both use HASH(CryptoID) for co-located JOINs. Join condition: CryptoID + BalanceDate.
cross_references.Hourly_WalletInventory_vs_Allocations: Hourly_WalletInventory = aggregated native-coin pool counts per CryptoID×WalletStatus (supply metrics, HASH(CryptoID)). Hourly_WalletAllocations = row-level recent allocation events per customer per crypto including ERC-20 (demand/activity data, HASH(GCID)). Different distributions, different ERC-20 coverage, different use cases.
cross_references.Hourly_RedeemActivity_links_Inventory: Hourly_RedeemActivity measures redemption drain (TotalRedeemUnits per CryptoID per day). JOIN to Hourly_WalletInventory on CryptoID to compare redemption rate against free inventory supply.
cross_references.Staking_ETH_domain_cluster: Four Staking_* tables form a coherent ETH staking domain: Staking_ETH_Rewards_Parameters (pool-level monthly parameters, 26 rows), Staking_WalletUserRewards (base user-month summaries, 23,617 rows), Staking_BI_Version_WalletUserRewards (BI-enriched user-month summaries with IsTestUser, 23,659 rows), Staking_BI_Version_ETH_Transactions (transaction-level detail with reward allocation, row count unknown). All frozen May 2023.
cross_references.EXW_InternalWallet_complements_Hourly_Om: EXW_InternalWallet (static metadata: wallet addresses, types, statuses for Gcid<=0 wallets) complements Hourly_OmnibusBalances (rolling balance snapshots for the same Gcid<=0 wallets). JOIN on WalletID+CryptoId to enrich omnibus balance data with wallet type and provider information.
cross_references.WalletDB_Staking_upstream_wikis: WalletDB.Staking schema has 5 wiki pages: Staking.Staking.md, Staking.StakingRewards.md, Staking.StakingTransactions.md, Staking.StakingStatuses.md, Staking.StakingExternalAddress.md. T1 coverage available for 8 columns in Staking_BI_Version_ETH_Transactions and 5 columns in both Staking_WalletUserRewards and Staking_BI_Version_WalletUserRewards.
cross_references.EXW_WalletLogins_vs_FCA_UserLogin: EXW_WalletLogins (all wallet users, HasWallet=1) and EXW_FCA_UserLogin (all wallet users) both capture login events from Fact_CustomerAction (ActionTypeID=14). EXW_FCA_UserLogin is a misnomer — it is NOT scoped to FCA-only. Difference may be in date range or SP parameterization; cross-reference to confirm exact scope overlap.
cross_references.Simplex_three_table_ecosystem: EXW_SimplexMapping + EXW_ECPBank + EXW_SimplexChargebacks form the complete Simplex payment ecosystem in EXW_dbo. EXW_SimplexMapping = provider API data (Simplex → eToro). EXW_ECPBank = acquirer settlement data (card network → ECP Bank → eToro). EXW_SimplexChargebacks = dispute/chargeback tracking. All frozen ~September 2022.
cross_references.EXW_PaymentReconciliation_vs_FactPayment: EXW_FactPayments (accumulating snapshot, 553K rows) and EXW_PaymentReconciliation (final-state join, 99K rows) are complementary views of the same payment universe. Use FactPayments for event history/status progressions; use PaymentReconciliation for current-state analysis or cross-source reconciliation (no JOIN needed between sources).
cross_references.Simplex_WalletDB_T1_chain: 16 T1 columns are shared between EXW_FactPayments and EXW_PaymentReconciliation — identical verbatim descriptions from WalletDB upstream wikis (Wallet.Payments, Wallet.PaymentTransactions, Wallet.PaymentStatuses). Any upstream wiki update to these 3 source tables must be reflected in both DWH wikis simultaneously.
